Modern.name: Modern
Modern.tagline: Clean lines, geometric shapes, and functional simplicity.
Modern.description: Originating in the late 19th century, modern design centers around form following function. It relies on clean, straight lines, simple geometric forms, and a strong rejection of heavy ornamentation.
Modern.idealLighting: Recessed spotlights, clean geometric pendant lights, and integrated LED strip lighting.
recommendedDecor: Monstera plants in clean ceramic pots

Minimalist.name: Minimalist
Minimalist.tagline: The art of 'Less is More' in luxury form.
Minimalist.description: Derived from traditional Japanese design, minimalism focuses on stripping elements down to their essential form, celebrating negative space, organic materials, and beautiful, diffused natural light.
Minimalist.idealLighting: Invisible cove lighting, warm diffused lamps, and sleek minimalist floor arches.

Luxury.name: Luxury
Luxury.tagline: Opulent materials, exquisite craftsmanship, and grand statements.
Luxury.description: Luxury interior design is defined by rich textures, high-end stone, metallic highlights, and custom-designed bespoke furniture that radiates sophistication and curated elegance.
Luxury.idealLighting: Bespoke crystal chandeliers, premium brass sconces, and dimmed spotlighting.
recommendedDecor: Scented luxury candles, velvet throw pillows

Contemporary.name: Contemporary
Contemporary.description: Unlike modern design, contemporary is fluid and refers to what is popular right now. It incorporates curved silhouettes, contrasting textures, and state-of-the-art technological integrations.
Contemporary.idealLighting: Artistic curved chandeliers, smart track lighting, and architectural accent fixtures.

Scandinavian.name: Scandinavian
Scandinavian.tagline: Warm, cozy, and intrinsically linked with nature (Hygge).
Scandinavian.description: Focuses on light-filled spaces, natural wood, cozy textiles, and functional layouts designed to make a space feel snug yet airy, bright, and deeply grounded in organic nature.
Scandinavian.idealLighting: Warm pendant lights, pleated table lamps, and flickering candlelight.
recommendedDecor: Light wood frames with nature photography

Industrial.name: Industrial
Industrial.tagline: Raw materials, exposed structural architecture, and urban edge.
Industrial.description: Inspired by converted warehouses and factories, industrial design proudly displays raw concrete, exposed pipes, steel beams, weathered wood, and leather elements.
Industrial.idealLighting: Edison bulb pendants, steel cage lamps, and robust articulating track lights.

Traditional.name: Traditional
Traditional.tagline: Rich history, symmetrical harmony, and timeless comfort.
Traditional.description: Grounded in European sensibilities, traditional design features elaborate wood carvings, rich color tapestries, matched pairs of furniture for symmetry, and classic historic patterns.
Traditional.idealLighting: Ornate brass lamps with silk shades, candle-style chandeliers, and glass sconces.
recommendedDecor: Oil paintings in carved gold frames

Bohemian.name: Bohemian
Bohemian.tagline: Wanderlust-inspired layers, rich plants, and free-spirited life.
Bohemian.description: Bohemian design is personal, exotic, and rules-free. It compiles textiles, global artifacts, rich macramé, layered vintage furniture, and an abundant tropical jungle of indoor plants.
Bohemian.idealLighting: Rattan woven pendants, Moroccan brass lanterns, and warm fairy lights.

Rustic.name: Rustic
Rustic.tagline: Honest, weathered textures and simple mountain-lodge warmth.
Rustic.description: rustic design emphasizes rugged, natural beauty. It highlights rough-hewn stone, unfinished wood planks, cozy fireplaces, and sturdy, comfortable seating made for simple gathering.
Rustic.idealLighting: Forged iron chandeliers, lantern-style fixtures, and warm wood table lamps.

Classic.name: Classic
Classic.tagline: Refined grandeur, balanced proportions, and royal restraint.
Classic.description: Inspired by Greek and Roman classicism, this style brings column details, symmetrical design structures, Greek-key patterns, and high-end marble fireplace mantels into modern homes.
Classic.idealLighting: Greek key detailed bronze lamps, neoclassical chandeliers, and picture frame lights.

Japanese.name: Japanese
Japanese.tagline: Zen balance, organic simplicity, and natural material cycles.
Japanese.description: Japanese design prioritizes meditative peace, shoji panel sliding doors, low-profile furniture seating, and natural cedar/bamboo surfaces. It represents natural breathing and tranquil living.
Japanese.idealLighting: Washi paper lamps (e.g. Noguchi style), soft hidden diffuse wood screens.
recommendedDecor: Zen stone water bowls, ceramic tea sets

Mediterranean.name: Mediterranean
Mediterranean.tagline: Sun-drenched stucco walls, cobalt seas, and warm clay tiled patios.
Mediterranean.description: Bringing the coastal charm of Italy, Greece, and Spain, this style utilizes whitewashed stucco, sea-cobalt accents, arched doorways, terracotta flooring, and gorgeous hand-painted ceramic tiles.
Mediterranean.idealLighting: Wrought-iron chandeliers, rustic clay lamps, and warm sunlight filtering through linen.
recommendedDecor: Olive tree branches in large vases

tips: Keep this zone highly open, clean, and spacious.
Ideal placement for cash registers, financial ledgers, and mirrors.
Decorate with blue, green, or silver color shades. Place water fountains here.
The most sacred zone. Perfect location for a mandir or silent yoga zone.
Avoid toilets, heavy storage cupboards, or kitchens in this zone.
Keep the walls light (cream, baby blue, off-white).
Ideal for social spaces. Let extensive natural morning sunlight stream inside.
Avoid high-profile heavy furniture right against the eastern walls.
Decorate with lush indoor plants, green elements, and wooden frames.
The ideal zone for hot appliances (Stoves, ovens, heaters, computer hardware).
Avoid water elements, toilets, or mirror installations here (extinguishes Fire energy).
Accentuate with beautiful red, orange, pink, or amber warm lamps.
Ideal zone for heavy furniture, main storage safes, or wardrobes.
Elevate flooring heights slightly or paint with warm terracotta or golden tones.
Perfect placement for large family or organizational legacy frames.
The zone of stability. Essential for masters of the house or directors of businesses.
Place the bed/desk here with head/chair facing North or East.
Keep this area heavy and grounded. Avoid any water tanks or entries here.
Excellent direction for overhead water drainage and dining gathering.
Add silver, blue, or deep charcoal styling overlays here.
Slightly heavier wood furniture works very well here.
The direction of movement. Place retail items here to facilitate quick sales.
Ideal for guest suites so they do not overstay, as energy moves frequently.
Add gorgeous light curtains, windchimes, or swirling silver metallic accents.

furniture.name: Low-Profile Ash Wood Sofa
furniture.reason:: Brings Japanese minimalism and a grounded, low seating posture to the layout.

furniture.name: Solid Oak Floating Sideboard
furniture.reason: Provides hidden storage that keeps the space completely neat and serene.

furniture.name: Round Hinoki Stave Coffee Table
furniture.reason: Organic curves soften the straight lines of the ash-wood sofa and cabinets.

furniture.name: Minimalist Origami Reading Chair
furniture.reason: Compact accent seat adding high sculptural design value without consuming visual volume.

lighting.name: Rice Paper Globe Pendant
lighting.placement: Centered above the low coffee table
lighting.purpose: Casts a warm, highly diffused, meditative glow over the seating assembly.

lighting.name: Integrated warm LED strip lights
lighting.placement: Concealed behind floating wood wall panels
lighting.purpose: Simulates indirect sunset lighting to create a soothing evening ambiance.

decor.name: Sisal & Wool Woven Flatweave Rug
decor.placement: Under the sofa assembly, running up to the credenza
decor.visualAppeal: Durable organic texture that matches the grounded raw tone of Japandi style.

decor.name: Bonsai Juniper on Timber Stand
decor.visualAppeal: A vibrant focal point of organic sculptured greenery that invites patience.

vastu.placementDetail: Placed securely against the South-West wall, facing East/North.
vastu.benefit: Provides high stability, mental clarity, and commanding authority during conversations.

vastu.placementDetail: Small stone tabletop fountain and the Bonsai placed in the East corner.
vastu.benefit: Vibrates with active morning solar rays, amplifying health and positive life vibrations.

spaceOptimizationTips: Hang shoeracks or cabinets off the floor (floating) to increase visible floor footprint.
Utilize sliding shoji screens or pocket doors instead of swinging wood doors to save 9 sq ft of sweep space.
